reasoning: Iteration 1 regressed due to critical zone map logic bug (inverted condition), causing 3 wrong rows despite apparent performance improvement. With 1 iteration remaining and 10.2x gap to DuckDB baseline, correctness must be fixed first. The iteration 1 evaluation identified viable lower-risk optimizations for iteration 2: Bloom filter for customer_ht probes, SIMD-accelerated lineitem hash join, and parallel aggregation merge. These avoid the failed zone map approach while targeting the dominant bottlenecks (orders_filter_join 50.7%, lineitem_scan_aggregation 32.3%).
focus_areas: Fix zone map logic bug (semantic correctness)
Implement Bloom filter for customer_ht probes to reduce hash table lookups
Apply SIMD acceleration to lineitem hash probing and date filtering
Optimize aggregation merge with parallel tree-based approach
strategy_notes: Iteration 1 regression analysis: The zone map concept was sound (could reduce orders_filter_join by 50-70%), but the skip condition at line 187 was inverted (skipped blocks where max_date < cutoff when filter is o_orderdate < cutoff; should skip blocks where min_date >= cutoff). For iteration 2, prioritize CORRECTNESS FIRST by fixing the zone map logic bug as a critical fix. Then apply conservative performance optimizations that are orthogonal to the zone map fix: (1) Bloom filter for customer_ht (low-risk, ~8-12ms savings in orders_filter_join by reducing hash table probe overhead), (2) SIMD-accelerated hash probing for lineitem_scan_aggregation using Abseil flat_hash_map or folly F14 (medium-risk, ~15-20ms savings via SSE/AVX2), (3) Parallel tree-based merge for aggregation_merge (low-risk, ~7-9ms savings). After these changes, performance should improve to ~170-185ms range (correctness-preserving). If time permits on next iteration, zone maps can be revisited with corrected logic. With 1 iteration left and 10.2x gap to DuckDB, even reaching ~170ms would be significant progress (5.5x improvement from baseline).
notes: Critical: Must fix zone map pruning logic bug as a semantic correctness fix before applying performance optimizations. The Bloom filter and SIMD optimizations are independent and can run in parallel. Avoid repeating the complex parallel zone map approach from iteration 1 until correctness is fully validated.
